ogTitle: Tecoma vs Teddywaddy West: Climate Safety

heading: Tecoma vs Teddywaddy West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tecoma, Victoria and Teddywaddy West, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Teddywaddy West has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Tecoma (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tecoma or Teddywaddy West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tecoma has a flood risk score of 42/100, while Teddywaddy West scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tecoma scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Teddywaddy West scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tecoma or Teddywaddy West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Teddywaddy West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
